text: Increased plasma estradiol leading to increased uterine weight is an established mechanism for onset of estrus cycling from reproductive maturation. Increased plasma estradiol and the timing of increased uterine weight as an adverse outcome have been studied in laboratory mammals by addition of various forms of estradiol (e.g. 17beta-estradiol, ethynyl estradiol; Laws et al. 2000) and toxicants (Li et al. 2008; Bo et al. 2022; Zhou et al. 2023). Studies involving doses of laboratory mammals with various forms of estradiol (e.g. 17beta-estradiol, ethynyl estradiol) are supportive of the mechanism of increases in exposure to estradiol compounds causing increased uterine weight at an earlier age.
